headline: Google Chrome deploys Gemini Nano AI model without user consent
body: Google Chrome is automatically installing a 4 GB Gemini Nano AI model on user devices without explicit consent, marking a significant shift in how AI infrastructure reaches end users. For healthcare organizations, this raises immediate concerns about data governance, device security, and compliance with HIPAA and other regulatory frameworks that govern patient information handling. If healthcare workers use Chrome to access EHRs, clinical decision support tools, or patient portals, the presence of an undisclosed local AI model introduces unpredictable security vectors and potential data leakage risks that IT teams must now actively monitor and mitigate. Healthcare IT leaders should treat this as an urgent inventory and risk assessment issue, requiring immediate guidance to clinical and administrative staff about browser usage policies and vendor-provided security controls that may no longer be sufficient.
